metadata.summary: A workflow to run cross-conditional models for genomics data.

documentation:
# Patches Patches takes as input a collection of observations or gene expression profiles obtained from multiple conditions referred to as groups -- for example, age, treatment or injury stage. Each group is characterized by a unique set of categories or attributes -- for example, 'young' or 'old' represent attributes of the group 'age'. Given an anndata object with metadata for the observations contained in separate columns of `anndata.obs`, Patches can: - Learn conditional and condition agnostic latent representations for observations. - Transfer observations across combinations of conditions. - Provide attribute specific scores for each gene, associating gene expression with effects of attributes.

## Table of Contents 1. [Installation](#installation) 2. [Repository structure](#repository-structure) 1. [Workflow API (for general users)](#workflow-api) 2. [Developer API (for advanced users)](#developer-api) 4. [Getting started](#getting-started) 5. [Release notes](#release-notes) 6. [Issues and contact](#issues-and-contact) 7. [License and citation](#license-and-citation) ## Installation - You will need Python 3.10 or newer in your system. If you don't have a Python environment setup, we recommend installing it through [Mambaforge](https://github.com/conda-forge/miniforge#mambaforge). - Install everything with: ``` pip install git+https://github.com/Computational-Morphogenomics-Group/Ladder.git@patches ``` ## Repository Structure Alongside Patches, this repository provides the low and high-level API's (called Ladder, still in development at time of Patches release) for running similar models with data from multiple conditions built on anndata. - All code is contained under the src directory. (add modules and stuff here) - Tests are contained under the tests directory. - All documentation can be generated and is serviced through the docs directory. ### Workflow API The high-level workflow API provides a very easy interface to apply models considered in the study ([scVI](https://scvi-tools.org/), [scANVI](https://scvi-tools.org/), Patches; all built on the Pyro framework) to multicondition datasets easily. - This is recommended for users that want to directly apply the model, without worrying much for technical details. - Consult the workflow docs for hyperparameters you can define and how to define them. ```python from ladder.data import get_data from ladder.scripts import CrossConditionWorkflow, InterpretableWorkflow # Workflow APIs, read docs on which one you'd like for your case! # Initialize workflow object workflow = InterpretableWorkflow(anndata, verbose=True, random_seed=42) # Define the condition classes & batch key to prepare the data factors = ["time", "age", "broad_type"] workflow.prep_model(factors, batch_key="sample", model_type='Patches', model_args={'ld_normalize' : True}) # Define model and optimizer hyperparams # Train the model workflow.run_model(max_epochs=2000, convergence_threshold=1e-5, convergence_window=2000) # Define convergence hyperparams ``` ### Developer API The low-level developer API explicitly performs all actions condensed into workflows. This allows for: - Implementing and running additional models using the Pyro framework. - Data preparation functions to provide ease for running the models and downstream visualizations. - Evaluation of generative accuracy alongside mixing/separation of conditional effects using defined metrics. This API is designed to be specifically used by those who are interested in specialized applications or those who would like to extend the codebase. Please use the issue tracker for any questions / encountered problems. ## Getting started Please check out the [documentation](https://ladder.readthedocs.io) for all instructions and tutorials. ## Release notes See the [changelog][changelog]. ## Issues and contact For additional questions, please reach out to: - ob2391@columbia.edu - bmd2151@columbia.edu If you encounter any bugs, please report it through the [issue tracker][issue-tracker]. In general, providing a minimally reproducible example is the best way to facilitate easy debugging of issues. ## License and citation This project is licensed under the terms of the MIT License. If the codebase has been of any use to you, please cite: > Beker, O., Amador, D., Nima, J., Deursen, S., Woappi, Y., & Dumitrascu, B. (2024). Patches: A Representation Learning framework for Decoding Shared and Condition-Specific Transcriptional Programs in Wound Healing. bioRxiv. BibTeX: ``` @article {Beker2024.12.23.630186, author = {Beker, Ozgur and Amador, Dreyton and Nima, Jose Pomarino and Deursen, Simon Van and Woappi, Yvon and Dumitrascu, Bianca}, title = {Patches: A Representation Learning framework for Decoding Shared and Condition-Specific Transcriptional Programs in Wound Healing}, elocation-id = {2024.12.23.630186}, year = {2024}, doi = {10.1101/2024.12.23.630186}, publisher = {Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory}, URL = {https://www.biorxiv.org/content/early/2024/12/24/2024.12.23.630186}, eprint = {https://www.biorxiv.org/content/early/2024/12/24/2024.12.23.630186.full.pdf}, journal = {bioRxiv} } ``` [issue-tracker]: https://github.com/Computational-Morphogenomics-Group/Ladder/issues [changelog]: https://ladder.readthedocs.io/latest/changelog.html [link-api]: https://ladder.readthedocs.io/latest/api.html [link-pypi]: https://pypi.org/project/scladder

modules.name: ladder.models.basics
documentation:
The basics module houses the private methods generally used for model definitions.

Although all methods are intended to be private, we provide documentation
for those who would like to construct their own models using these helpers.

functions.name: ladder.models.basics._split_in_half
documentation:
Function to split a tensor in half.

Courtesy of: <https://pyro.ai/examples/scanvi.html>

# Parameters

t

: Tensor to be split

# Returns

ts

: Size 2 tuple of tensors that are the halves of the original {class}`~torch.Tensor`.

functions.name: ladder.models.basics._broadcast_inputs
documentation:
Helper for broadcasting shapes.

Courtesy of: <https://pyro.ai/examples/scanvi.html>

# Parameters

input_args

: Array-like of {class}`~torch.Tensor` to broadcast.

# Returns

input_args

: Array-like of {class}`~torch.Tensor` that includes broadcasted tensors.

functions.name: ladder.models.basics._make_fc
documentation:
Helper to make FC layers in quick succession for hidden layers.

Courtesy of: <https://pyro.ai/examples/scanvi.html>

# Parameters

dims

: Array-like of {class}`int` specifying the sizes for layers. `dims[0], dims[-1]` are input and output respectively.

keep_last_batch_norm

: Argument to decide whether to keep last BatchNorm1d layer.

# Returns

layers

: The layers packed into a single module.

classes.name: ladder.models.basics._make_func

methods.name: ladder.models.basics._make_func._zinb_forward
documentation: # Forwards for different configs

Helper to make functions for variational posteriors. Inherits {class}`~torch.nn.Module`

Wraps around {func}`_make_fc` for various distribution configurations
to reduce redundancy when defining the actual models.

# Parameters

in_dims

: Size of the input layer.

hidden_dims

: 1D Array-like of `int`. Includes sizes for intermediate layers.

out_dim

: Size of the output layer.

last_config

: The parameterization that is expected by `dist_config`.

dist_config

: The distribution for the parameter that corresponds to the modelled layer.

keep_last_batch_norm

: Argument to decide whether to keep last BatchNorm1d layer. Passed to {function}`_make_fc`.

# Notes

The value for `dist_config` inherently determines the value for `last_config`, but they
should be manually provided specifically. This helps readability in model definitions to
make sure that the parameterizations are correct.
Helper to make functions between variables

modules.name: ladder.scripts.visuals
documentation: The visuals module houses the visualizers for workflows.

functions.name: ladder.scripts.visuals._plot_loss
documentation: Visualizer to plot model losses

modules.name: ladder.scripts.training
documentation:
The training module includes the functions used to train the models.

The functions defined here can be run independently for low-level
specific applications, or through the workflows API for high-level
standard applications.

functions.name: ladder.scripts.training.get_device
documentation:
Prints currently used device.

Returns
-------
torch.device
    Device object.
Helper to get device

functions.name: ladder.scripts.training.train_pyro
documentation:
Runner for basic Pyro models.

Trains up to `num_epochs` or until a new minimum is not attained
that is lower than the older minimum by `convergence_threshold` for
`convergence_window` epochs.

# Parameters

model

: The model to train.

train_loader

: Data loader for the training set.

test_loader

: Data loader for the test set.

num_epochs

: Maximum number of epochs to run.

convergence_threshold

: Minimum improvement to decide on convergence.

convergence_window

: Patience window for deciding on convergence.

verbose

: If `True`, prints out the loss at every epoch.

device

: Device object to run models on.

optim_args

: Arguments to be passed to `` :class:`pyro.optim.MultiStepLR `` for fine tuning if needed.

# Returns

model

: The model object post-training.

loss_track_train

: {class}`float` array containing the training loss per epoch.

loss_track_test

: {class}`float` array containing the test loss per epoch.
Helper to train Pyro models

functions.name: ladder.scripts.training.train_pyro_disjoint_param
documentation:
Runner for Patches, but can be used for other adversarial models.

Trains up to `num_epochs` or until a new minimum is not attained
that is lower than the older minimum by `convergence_threshold` for
`convergence_window` epochs. Allows for setting different training routines
for the adversarial loss.

# Parameters

model

: The model to train.

train_loader

: Data loader for the training set.

test_loader

: Data loader for the test set.

num_epochs

: Maximum number of epochs to run.

convergence_threshold

: Minimum improvement to decide on convergence.

convergence_window

: Patience window for deciding on convergence.

verbose

: If `True`, prints out the loss at every epoch.

device

: Device object to run models on.

warmup

: Number of epochs to run the classifier before running the entire model.

classifier_aggression

: Number of epochs the classifier takes independently between jointly trained epochs.

optim_args

: Arguments to be passed to `` :class:`torch.optim.lr_scheduler.MultiStepLR `` for fine tuning if needed.

# Returns

model

: The model object post-training.

loss_track_train

: {class}`float` array containing the training loss per epoch.

loss_track_test

: {class}`float` array containing the test loss per epoch.

params_nonc_names

: {class}`str` set containing model parameter names except the classifier.

params_c_names

: {class}`str` set containing model parameter names for the classifier.
Helper to train models that involve disjoint parameters during training

modules.name: ladder.scripts.metrics
documentation:
The metrics module houses functions for evaluation.

The functions here are used to assess latent space conditional
mixing / separation and generative accuracy for point clouds.
The generative accuracy metrics are divided into two groups depending
on how they operate. (1) uses traditional regression metrics on pseudobulk
sequence-depth normalized profiles, (2) uses metrics to assess accuracy
of point clouds.

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._get_iterator
documentation: Get tqdm iterator

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._norm_lib_size
documentation: _get_normalized_profile implicitly calls this

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._get_normalized_profile
documentation: Public version internally calls this

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._get_idxs
documentation: Get indexes where points belong to target
_get_subset implicitly calls this

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._get_subset
documentation: Subset the dataset where target is included

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._get_rmse_n_to_1
documentation: Compare multiple profiles to a single mean profile - RMSE

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._get_corr_n_to_1
documentation: Compare multiple profiles to a single mean profile - Pearson Correlation

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._get_chamf_n_to_1
documentation: Compare multiple point clouds to a single cloud - CD

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._get_sliced_wasserstein_n_to_1
documentation: Compare multiple point clouds to a single cloud - 2-SW

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics.get_normalized_profile
documentation:
Used to get library-size normalized pseudobulk profile from group of cells.

The normalized profile can then be compared to other profiles through RMSE
or Pearson Correlation. Note that we cannot do this with the original data points
if there is no explicit matching (as in the case of transfer).

# Parameters

point_dataset

: The {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` object to be provided.

target

: Optional target condition encoding tensor to subset the provided `TensorDataset` input.

lib_size

: Library size to normalize each cell.

batched

: If `True`, assumes batch is concatenated to the inputs

# Returns

normalized_profile

: Normalized pseudo-bulk profile for the given points.
### Reconstruction - Functions ####

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics.gen_profile_reproduction
documentation:
Used to generate new points by performing a full pass through the models.

The points and their normalized profiles are logged for each generation, up to the
number of `n_trials`. If `source` and `target` are provided, performs a transfer. Otherwise
runs for reconstruction.

# Parameters

point_dataset

: The {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` object to be provided.

model

: The model generator function wrapped in {class}`~pyro.infer.predictive.Predictive`.

source

: Optional source condition encoding tensor to subset the provided `TensorDataset` input.

target

: Optional target conditon encoding tensor to subset the provided `TensorDataset` input.

n_trials

: Number of times to repeat the generative process. Must be positive.

lib_size

: Library size for normalized profiles.

verbose

: If `True`, prints how many iterations are left for `n_trials`.

use_cuda

: If `True`, attempts to use CUDA device for the generative process.

# Returns

profiles

: Normalized pseudo-bulk profiles for the points generated.

preds

: Points generated by the model.

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics.get_reproduction_error
documentation:
Calculates the model generative error for the given metric.

Wraps around {func}`gen_profile_reproduction` with the\`\_metric_func\`
corresponding to the chosen metric.

# Parameters

point_dataset

: The {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` object to be provided.

model

: The model generator function wrapped in {class}`~pyro.infer.predictive.Predictive`.

source

: Optional source condition encoding tensor to subset the provided {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` input.

target

: Optional target conditon encoding tensor to subset the provided {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` input.

metric

: Metric to be considered for evaluation.

n_trials

: Number of times to repeat the generative process. Must be positive.

lib_size

: Library size for normalized profiles.

batched

: If `True`, assumes batch is concatenated to the inputs.

kwargs

: Additional keyword arguments to be passed to {func}`gen_profile_reproduction`.

# Returns

preds_mean_error

: Mean of the value for the metric calculated across `n_trials` repetitions.

preds_var_error

: Variance of the value for the metric calculated across `n_trials` repetitions.

pred_profiles

: Normalized pseudo-bulk profiles for the points generated.

preds

: Points generated by the model.

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._prep_label_data
documentation: ###### MIXING - FUNCTIONS #########

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics.knn_error
documentation:
Calculates KNN classifier accuracy on embeddings.

Trains a KNN classifier on the `test_for` column of {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` using the embeddings `embed` located at {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obsm`.

# Parameters

anndata

: The anndata object provided.

test_for

: Factor column in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` for the original labels.

embed

: Key in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obsm` pointing to the embeddings to run K-Means with.

n_neighbors

: K for the KNN classifier.

# Returns

{class}`float`

: Accuracy of the KNN classifier.
KNN Classifier Error with euclidean distance

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics._run_k_means
documentation: Run k-means for nmi / ari

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics.kmeans_nmi
documentation:
Calculates the K-Means NMI for latent representations.

The K-Means clustering in the latents is compared with the labels
originally appearing in {class}`~anndata.AnnData`.

# Parameters

anndata

: The anndata object provided.

test_for

: Factor column in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` for the original labels.

embed

: Key in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obsm` pointing to the embeddings to run K-Means with.

# Returns

float

: NMI for original labels and K-Means clusters.

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics.kmeans_ari
documentation:
Calculates the K-Means ARI for latent representations.

The K-Means clustering in the latents is compared with the labels
originally appearing in {class}`~anndata.AnnData`.

# Parameters

anndata

: The anndata object provided.

test_for

: Factor column in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` for the original labels.

embed

: Key in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obsm` pointing to the embeddings to run K-Means with.

# Returns

float

: ARI for original labels and K-Means clusters.

functions.name: ladder.scripts.metrics.calc_asw
documentation:
Calculates the normalized Average Silhouette Width (ASW).

Courtesy of scib (2021, 10.1038/s41592-021-01336-8)
Source: <https://github.com/theislab/scib/blob/main/scib/metrics/silhouette.py>

# Parameters

anndata

: The anndata object provided.

test_for

: Factor column in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` for the original labels.

embed

: Key in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obsm` pointing to the embeddings to run K-Means with.

# Returns

float

: Average Silhouette Width normalized to the range [0,1].

modules.name: ladder.data.builtin_data
documentation:
The builtin_data module houses the functions that are necessary for tutorials.

The data provided in this module are needed for specific tutorials, and
are a good place to start when learning the modules.

functions.name: ladder.data.builtin_data.get_data
documentation:
Used to download data for tutorials.

# Parameters

dataset

: Specifies which dataset is to be downloaded.

save_path

: Specifies the directory in which the dataset will be saved. Defaults to `./data/`.

smoke_test

: Used when testing to pass through without actually unpacking the response from server.

# Returns

None

modules.name: ladder.scripts.workflows
documentation:
The workflows module houses the workflow API.

The workflow API is intended as a high-level API for users to
generally apply the models included in `ladder` to their datasets.
It includes standard applications of the models defined within.

classes.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.__init__
documentation: Constructor

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.__str__
documentation: Printer

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.__repr__
documentation: Repr

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow._prep_data
documentation: Data preparation, implicitly called by prep_model
For specific functions see the related module

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow._fetch_model_args
documentation: Set up model args

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow._clear_bad_optim_args
documentation: Delete unused optimizer args

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow._register_latent_dims
documentation: Register latent dims to attributes

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.prep_model
documentation:
Prepares the model to be run.

The choice of model implicitly decides the kind of condition encodings
to use, so there is no need to have a separate data preparation.

# Parameters

factors

: Factors from {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` to register to the model.

batch_key

: Defines the workflow to be used. Affects model structure. Can later be accessed with same named attribute.

cell_type_label_key

: Optional cell type labels in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs`, required if cell-type specific evaluation is desired.

minibatch_size

: Size of the minibatch to be provided during training.

model_type

: Specifies the model attached to the current workflow.

model_args

: Model arguments passed to low-level model constructor. See {mod}`~ladder.models` for details.

optim_args

: Optimizer arguments passed to low-level trainer. See {mod}`~ladder.scripts.training` for details.

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.run_model
documentation:
Runs the model on the attached data object.

# Parameters

max_epochs

: Maximum number of epochs to run.

convergence_threshold

: Minimum improvement required to continue training.

convergence_window

: Number of epochs to wait until a new minimum is attained.

classifier_warmup

: Number of epochs to run the classifier before running the entire model.

classifier_aggression

: Number of epochs the classifier takes independently between jointly trained epochs. Used for Patches.

params_save_path

: If provided, saves the model to the specified path.

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.save_model
documentation:
Saves the attached model.

# Parameters

params_save_path

: Path to save model parameters. Expects only the name without extensions.

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.load_model
documentation:
Loads parameters for the attached model. Needs {meth}`prep_model` to be run first.

# Parameters

params_load_path

: Path to find model parameters. Expects only the shared prefix, and not the trailing "\_torch.pth" or "\_pyro.pth".

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.plot_loss
documentation:
Simple plotter for loss functions.

# Parameters

save_loss_path

: If provided, saves the figure to the specified location. Requires the full name with extensions (eg. fig.png).

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.write_embeddings
documentation:
Places the calculated cell embeddings from the trained model under the corresponding {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obsm` field.

Each model has a separate name for their respective latent, so that more than a
single workflow running on the same object instance does not overwrite info.

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow._subset_by_type
documentation: Subset the test set to a single type

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.evaluate_reconstruction
documentation:
Evaluates the quality of reconstructions with generative metrics.

# Parameters

subset

: Key from {attr}`~BaseWorkflow.levels` to subset cells for a specific condition before evaluating reconstruction.

cell_type

: Requires {attr}`~BaseWorkflow.cell_type_label_key` to be defined as attribute. Subset cells to a single type before evaluating reconstruction.

n_iter

: Number of times to repeat the generative process.
Evaluate the overall reconstruction error

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.evaluate_separability
documentation:
Evaluates the separability of latent embeddings for conditions.

# Parameters

factor

: Item listed in {attr}`BaseWorkflow.factors`. If not provided, the metrics will be evaluated on the combinations of factors.

class_variables.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.OPT_CLASS1
documentation: These do not require a complex optimizer loop

class_variables.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.BaseWorkflow.OPT_CLASS2
documentation: These require adversarial optimizer for the latent

Base class for all workflows.

Offers a high-level API that does not require running blocks of
code in quick succession, as the process for each dataset is more or
less similar. Must not be instantiated and used directly. All parameters
given to specific functions throughout the workflow can later be accessed with
the same named attribute.

# Parameters

anndata

: The dataset object to be used throughout the analyses.

config

: Defines the workflow to be used. Affects model structure.

verbose

: If `True`, prints progress messages for various methods within the module.

random_seed

: If given, seeds the internal modules with the value.

# Attributes

anndata

: The attached {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` object.

batch_key

: Optional batch key in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` for correction.

batch_mapping

: Mapping of batch literals to encodings, only appears if batch key is provided in workflow.

cell_type_label_key

: Optional cell type labels in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs`, required if cell-type specific evaluation is desired.

config

: The config string provided during construction.

converter

: Low-level converter class for the attached {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset`. See `` :func:`~ladder.data.real_data.distrib_dataset `` for details.

dataset

: Low-level {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` object passed to the model. See `` :func:`~ladder.data.real_data.distrib_dataset `` for details.

factors

: List of factors to register to the model.

verbose

: If `True`, prints progress messages for various methods within the module.

random_seed

: If given, seeds the internal modules with the value.

label_style

: Defines the conditional encoding style to use depending on the model.

latent_dim

: Size of the latent dimension for the model. Common latent for Patches.

len_attrs

: Specifies the number of attributes per condition class.

levels

: Mapping of condition literals to encodings.

l_mean

: If {attr}`batch_key` is provided in workflow, the empirical library size log-mean for each batch (1-D Array-like of {class}`float`). A single value otherwise.

l_scale

: If {attr}`batch_key` is provided in workflow, then the empirical library size log-variance for each batch (1-D Array-like of {class}`float`). A single value otherwise.

minibatch_size

: Size of the minibatch to be provided during training.

model

: The model object attached to the workflow.

model_type

: Specifies the model attached to the current workflow.

optim_args

: Optimizer arguments passed to low-level trainer. See {mod}`~ladder.scripts.training` for details.

predictive

: Low-level generator to be used for tasks after training.

reconstruction

: Defines the decoder to be used.

train_loss

: {class}`~numpy.ndarray` of losses recorded on the training set during training.

train_set

: Low-level training {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` passed to the model. See `` :func:`~ladder.data.real_data.distrib_dataset `` for details.

test_loss

: {class}`~numpy.ndarray` of losses recorded on the test set during training.

test_set

: Low-level test {class}`~torch.utils.data.Dataset` passed to the model. See `` :func:`~ladder.data.real_data.distrib_dataset `` for details.

w_dim

: Size of conditional latents, only defined for Patches.

# Methods

prep_model(factors, batch_key=None, cell_type_label_key=None, minibatch_size=128, model_type="Patches", model_args=None, optim_args=None)

: Prepares the model to be run.

run_model(max_epochs=1500, convergence_threshold=1e-3, convergence_window=30, classifier_warmup=0, params_save_path=None)

: Runs the model on the attached data object.

save_model(params_save_path)

: Saves the attached model.

load_model(params_load_path)

: Loads parameters for the attached model. Needs {meth}`prep_model` to be run first.

plot_loss()

: Simple plotter for loss functions.

write_embeddings()

: Places the calculated cell embeddings from the trained model under the corresponding {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obsm` field.

evaluate_reconstruction(subset=None, cell_type=None, n_iter=5)

: Evaluates the quality of reconstructions with generative metrics.

evaluate_separability(factor=None)

: Evaluates the separability of the latent encodings with respect to conditional effects.

classes.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.InterpretableWorkflow

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.InterpretableWorkflow.__init__
documentation: Constructor

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.InterpretableWorkflow.get_conditional_loadings
documentation:
Writes attribute specific gene loadings to {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.var`.

Only to be used with Patches, as the other models do not offer
an attribute-specific way to learn coefficients.

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.InterpretableWorkflow.get_common_loadings
documentation:
Writes non-conditional gene loadings to {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.var`.

Can be used with all models.

Interpretable workflow for training with a linear decoder.

Inherits {class}`BaseWorkflow` and adds functionalities desired from
running the interpretable models with linear decoders.

# Parameters

anndata

: The dataset object to be used throughout the analyses.

verbose

: If `True`, prints progress messages for various methods within the module.

random_seed

: If given, seeds the internal modules with the value.

# Methods

get_conditional_loadings()

: Writes attribute specific gene loadings to {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.var`.

get_common_loadings()

: Writes non-conditional gene loadings to {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.var`.

classes.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.CrossConditionWorkflow

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.CrossConditionWorkflow.__init__
documentation: Constructor

methods.name: ladder.scripts.workflows.CrossConditionWorkflow.evaluate_transfer
documentation:
Evaluates the quality of transfers with generative metrics.

# Parameters

source

: Key from {attr}`BaseWorkflow.levels` to decide source condition.

target

: Key from {attr}`BaseWorkflow.levels` to decide target condition.

cell_type

: Requires {attr}`BaseWorkflow.cell_type_label_key` to be defined as attribute. Subset cells to a single type before evaluating transfer.

n_iter

: Number of times to repeat the generative process.
Evaluate transfers for conditions

Cross-condition workflow for training with a non-linear decoder.

Inherits {class}`BaseWorkflow` and adds functionalities desired from
running a cross-conditional model for more precise reconstructions and transfers.

# Parameters

anndata

: The dataset object to be used throughout the analyses.

verbose

: If `True`, prints progress messages for various methods within the module.

random_seed

: If given, seeds the internal modules with the value.

# Methods

evaluate_transfer(source, target, cell_type=None, n_iter=10)

: Evaluates the quality of transfers with generative metrics.

modules.name: ladder.data.real_data
documentation:
The real_data module houses the functions to be used with real datasets.

The functions defined here can either be used independently for
specific low-level applications or through the workflows API for
high-level, standard applications.

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data._process_batch_dd
documentation: Batch processing for distrib_dataset

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data._process_batch_cb
documentation: Batch processing for construct_labels

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data._get_idxs
documentation: Get subset indices from cloud

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data._get_subset
documentation: Get the actual subset object from cloud

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data._concat_cat_df
documentation: Helper to convert metadata from DataFrame to torch object

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data._factors_to_col
documentation: Construct combinations of attributes from condition classes

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data._process_array
documentation: Check to make sure array is dense

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data.preprocess_anndata
documentation:
Function to preprocess the inputted {class}`~anndata.AnnData` object.

Does not do any critical modifications, but instead ensures that the numerical
columns in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` are of type {class}`float` and string columns are defined
as {class}`~pandas.CategoricalDtype`.

# Parameters

anndat

: The {class}`~anndata.AnnData` object to be preprocessed.

# Returns

anndat

: Object after typecasting operations.
Simple preprocessing to conver to anndata

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data.construct_labels
documentation:
Function to generate conditional labels for the various models included.

# Parameters

counts

: The field corresponding to {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.X`.

metadata

: The field corresponding to {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs`.

factors

: 1D Array-like of {class}`str`. The list specifying factors, which are names of the columns from {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs`.

style

: Specifies the label encoding.

batch_key

: Specifies the batch key, must be included in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs`.

# Returns

dataset

: The dataset object to be used downstream.

levels

: A mapping between the literal combinations of `factors` and their numerical equivalents.

converter

: The converter object with the associated dataset.

batch_mapping

: Returned only if `batch_key` is given. Ordinal encoding for the batch dimension.

functions.name: ladder.data.real_data.distrib_dataset
documentation:
Function that distributes the {class}`~torch.utils.data.TensorDataset` generated by `construct_labels`.

# Parameters

dataset

: The `dataset` output from `construct_labels`.

levels

: The `levels` output from `construct_labels`.

split_pcts

: Size 2 list of `float` specifying the proportions for training and test respectively. Ignored if both `keep_train` and `keep_test` are not `None`.

batch_size

: Mini-batch size for the models to train on.

keep_train

: 1D Array-like of `str`. Specifies the levels to keep in the training dataset. Elements must be from `levels.keys()`.

keep_test

: 1D Array-like of `str`. Specifies the levels to keep in the test dataset. Elements must be from `levels.keys()`.

batch_key

: Must not be `None` if `batch_key` was previously provided to `construct_labels`. The actual values is unimportant for this scope.

kwargs

: Keyword arguments passed to `utils.DataLoader`.

# Returns

train_set

: The full training set to be used downstream.

test_set

: The full test set to be used downstream.

train_loader

: The corresponding loader for `train_set`.

test_loader

: The corresponding loader for `test_set`.

l_mean

: If `batch_key` is provided, the empirical library size log-mean for each batch (1-D Array-like of {class}`float`). A single value otherwise.

l_scale

: If `batch_key` is provided, then the empirical library size log-variance for each batch (1-D Array-like of {class}`float`). A single value otherwise.
Helper to go from dataset to train-test split loaders

classes.name: ladder.data.real_data.MetadataConverter

methods.name: ladder.data.real_data.MetadataConverter.map_to_df
documentation:
The function that actually does the metadata mapping.

# Parameters

met_val_string

: Tensor shaped like {class}`~torch.utils.data.TensorDataset`.

# Returns

metadata_mapping

: Numerical tensor converted back to categorical equivalent.

Class used to convert numerical torch tensors into categorical equivalents.

Allows mapping of numerical tensors with information that would usually be expected
in {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` into categorical equivalent with literals from the original metadata.
This class allows for arbitrary subsets of data to be mapped back.

# Parameters

metadata

: The dataframe object that includes the metadata, which is {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` for most cases.

# Attributes

df_view

: Dataframe object for reference.

num_cols

: Number of columns for `df_view`.

classes.name: ladder.data.real_data.AnndataConverter

methods.name: ladder.data.real_data.AnndataConverter.map_to_anndat
documentation:
Function to actually do the mapping to {class}`~anndata.AnnData`.

# Parameters

val_tup

: Size 3 tuple of {class}`~torch.Tensor`. The first index is used for counts. The second index
  provides labels, which are not used here as they are redundant but required for training.
  The third index provides the numerically encoded metadata.

# Returns

anndat

: The anndata equivalent of the numerical {class}`~torch.Tensor` objects.

Class used to convert tensor datasets into an {class}`~anndata.AnnData` object.

This class allows for arbitrary subsets of tensors to be mapped back into
an object that looks like the subset of the original object. Inherits
{class}`MetadataConverter`.

# Parameters

metadata_df

: The dataframe object that includes the metadata, which is {attr}`~anndata.AnnData.obs` for most cases.

classes.name: ladder.data.real_data.ConcatTensorDataset
documentation:
Allows for arbitrary concatenation of {class}`~torch.utils.data.TensorDataset`.

Courtesy of <https://github.com/johann-petrak/pytorch/commit/eb70e81e31508c383bdc17059ddb532a6b40468c>
ConcatDataset of TensorDatasets which supports getting slices and index lists/arrays.
This dataset allows the use of slices, e.g. ds[2:4] and of arrays or lists of multiple indices
if all concatenated datasets are either TensorDatasets or Subset or other ConcatTensorDataset instances
which eventually contain only TensorDataset instances. If no slicing is needed,
this class works exactly like torch.utils.data.ConcatDataset and can concatenate arbitrary
(not just TensorDataset) datasets.

# Parameters

> datasets
>
> : List of datasets to be concatenated.

modules.name: ladder.models.scvi_variants
documentation:
The scvi_variants module houses the model definitions that are based on the scVI (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41592-018-0229-2) skeleton.

All model implementations are available through Pyro.

methods.name: ladder.models.scvi_variants.SCVI.save
documentation:
Saves model parameters to disk.

# Parameters

path

: Path to save model parameters.
Save self

methods.name: ladder.models.scvi_variants.SCVI.load
documentation:
Loads model parameters from disk.

# Parameters

path

: Path to find model parameters. Should not include the extensions `_torch.pth` or `_pyro.pth` or any such variant.

map_location

: Specifies where the model should be loaded. See {class}`~torch.device` for details.
Load

scVI (<https://www.nature.com/articles/s41592-018-0229-2>), implemented through `pyro`.

# Parameters

num_genes

: Size of the gene space.

l_loc

: Either a single value for log-mean library size, or a 1D array-like of values if `batch_correction`.

l_scale

: Either a single value for log-variance library size, or a 1D array-like of values if `batch_correction`.

hidden_dim

: Size of the hidden layers throughout the model.

num_layers

: Number of hidden layers between any input and output layer.

latent_dim

: Size of the latent variable `z`.

scale_factor

: Factor used to scale and normalize the loss.

batch_correction

: If `True`, expects batch to be appended to input and corrects for batch.

reconstruction

: The distribiution assumed to model the input data.

# Methods

\_\_init\_\_(num_genes, l_loc, l_scale, hidden_dim=128, num_layers=2, latent_dim=10, scale_factor=1.0, batch_correction=False, reconstruction="ZINB")

: Constructor for scVI.

model(x, y=None)

: Generative model for scVI.

guide(x, y=None)

: Approximate variational posterior for scVI.

generate(x, y_source=None, y_target=None)

: Function used post-training for Oracle-scVI to facilitate transfer between conditional labels.

get_weights()

: Returns interpretable coefficients for latents.

save(path="scvi_params")

: Saves model parameters to disk.

load(path="scvi_params", map_location=None)

: Loads model parameters from disk.

methods.name: ladder.models.scvi_variants.SCANVI.load
documentation:
Loads model parameters from disk.

# Parameters

path

: Path to find model parameters. Should not include the extensions `_torch.pth` or `_pyro.pth` or any such variant.

map_location

: Specifies where the model should be loaded. See {class}`~torch.device` for details.
Load

Supervised scANVI (<https://www.embopress.org/doi/full/10.15252/msb.20209620>), implemented through `pyro`.

# Parameters

num_genes

: Size of the gene space.

l_loc

: Either a single value for log-mean library size, or a 1D array-like of values if `batch_correction`.

l_scale

: Either a single value for log-variance library size, or a 1D array-like of values if `batch_correction`.

num_labels

: Length of the one-hot encoded condition labels expected in the data.

hidden_dim

: Size of the hidden layers throughout the model.

num_layers

: Number of hidden layers between any input and output layer.

latent_dim

: Size of the latent variable `z`.

alpha

: Factor used to scale the classifier loss.

scale_factor

: Factor used to scale and normalize the loss.

batch_correction

: If `True`, expects batch to be appended to input and corrects for batch.

reconstruction

: The distribiution assumed to model the input data.

# Methods

\_\_init\_\_(num_genes, l_loc, l_scale, num_labels, hidden_dim=128, num_layers=2, latent_dim=10, alpha=1.0, scale_factor=1.0, batch_correction=False, reconstruction="ZINB")

: Constructor for supervised scANVI.

model(x, y)

: Generative model for supervised scANVI.

guide(x, y)

: Approximate variational posterior for supervised scANVI.

generate(x, y_source, y_target)

: Function used post-training for supervised scANVI to facilitate transfer between conditional labels.

get_weights()

: Returns interpretable coefficients for latents.

save(path="scanvi_params")

: Saves model parameters to disk.

load(path="scanvi_params", map_location=None)

: Loads model parameters from disk.
# scANVI taken from https://pyro.ai/examples/scanvi.html

methods.name: ladder.models.scvi_variants.Patches.load
documentation:
Loads model parameters from disk.

# Parameters

path

: Path to find model parameters. Should not include the extensions `_torch.pth` or `_pyro.pth` or any such variant.

map_location

: Specifies where the model should be loaded. See {class}`~torch.device` for details.
Load

Patches, the multi-attribute model.

# Parameters

num_genes

: Size of the gene space.

l_loc

: Either a single value for log-mean library size, or a 1D array-like of values if `batch_correction`.

l_scale

: Either a single value for log-variance library size, or a 1D array-like of values if `batch_correction`.

num_labels

: Total number of attributes present in the data.

len_attrs

: 1D Array-like of `int`. Specifies how many attributes per condition class.

betas

: Scales the adversarial & classifier loss for each condition class.

w_loc

: Means for the conditionally selected multivariate gaussians to parameterize each `w_k`.

w_scale

: Stds for the conditionally selected multivariate gaussians to parameterize each `w_k`.

w_dim

: Size of each `w_k` for the attributes.

w_kl

: KL weight for conditional latents.

latent_dim

: Size of the latent variable `z`.

z_kl

: KL weight for common latent.

recon_weight

: Weight for reconstructin error.

num_layers

: Number of hidden layers between any input and output layer.

hidden_dim

: Size of the hidden layers throughout the model.

scale_factor

: Factor used to scale and normalize the loss.

batch_correction

: If `True`, expects batch to be appended to input and corrects for batch.

ld_sparsity

: If `True`, adds L1 loss for attribute specific latents. Can only be used with linear decoders.

ld_normalize

: If `True`, adds bias term to decoder. Can only be used with linear decoders.

sparsity_lambda

: Weight of the L1 term in the loss.

reconstruction

: The distribiution assumed to model the input data.

# Methods

\_\_init\_\_(num_genes, l_loc, l_scale, num_labels, len_attrs, betas=None, w_loc=None, w_scale=None, w_dim=2, latent_dim=10, num_layers=2, hidden_dim=128, scale_factor=1.0, batch_correction=False, ld_sparsity=False, ld_normalize=False, reconstruction="ZINB")

: Constructor for Patches.

model(x, y)

: Generative model for Patches.

guide(x, y)

: Approximate variational posterior for Patches.

adversarial(x,y)

: Adversarial loss for Patches.

generate(x, y_source, y_target)

: Function used post-training for Patches to transfer between conditional labels.

get_weights()

: Returns interpretable coefficients for latents.

save(path="scanvi_params")

: Saves model parameters to disk.

load(path="scanvi_params", map_location=None)

: Loads model parameters from disk.
